steps.title: Map the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 makes a general claim: Spartans kept their laconic wit even when in peril. What follows the blank is a specific historical incident (Philip II threatening Laconia, Spartans replying with one word). So the transition must introduce a concrete example of the general claim.

steps.title: Match a transition that introduces an example
steps.content: "For instance" is the standard way to introduce a specific illustration of a general statement, which is exactly the move this passage makes.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Rather,: signals correction or a contrast with what came before, but the second sentence agrees with and illustrates the first, not corrects it. ✗
  • Choice B — In other words,: signals a restatement of the same idea in different words, but the Philip II story is a new event, not a rephrasing. ✗
  • Choice C — That said,: signals concession or contrast, but nothing is being qualified or pushed back against. ✗
  • Choice D — For instance,: correctly introduces the Philip II anecdote as a specific example of laconic wit under peril. ✓
That is the correct answer.
